sentences.text: Hey, Jessie.
Can you hear me?
Ok.

I'm good.
I'm good.

The conference rooms are all booked up.
I hope you can hear me.
Ok.
I had to duck into a coffee shop that is serving lunch.
No.
Yeah, I can hear you perfectly fine.
Terrific.
All right.
Um Well, thank you so much for joining.
Uh I wanted to quickly kind of check in and see how progress has been.
Make sure it's aligning with what you're expecting and then highlight a couple of things that are coming uh in an apartment like you that you guys out of RK will get access to.
So just get you excited for some new product coming in.
And of course, give you the opportunity to let me know what feedback you're hearing or if there's anything else that we should be uh you know, taking a look at on our end.
Ok, perfect.
Excellent.
I will do a quick screen share, let's see, share desktop and let's get started.
So we'll do our usual success planning.
We'll touch base on that a little bit to see if we need to make any changes, update anything.
I'll show you some usage stats that you can feel good about who's logging in, who's what surveys are being used the most.
Because I know we kind of talked about some of the portfolio needing to have a little bit more attention.
We'll talk about any feedback you have and then in the next steps area, we'll also, I'll highlight some of the additional product that's coming.
Sounds good.
Sounds good.
All right.
So jumping right into success planning, I pulled this, of course, from the conversations we had, I believe that was back in April.
So I know some of our key business goals for RK properties is just to kind of drive that top of the market demand, the best rent pricing.
Anything else you'd add to this or is there anything we could flush out here uh across these business goals and objectives?
What is the key metric this summer?
For instance, how are you guys thinking about having a successful summer?
Yeah, obviously, I think probably for all of your clients having occupancy up and keeping our rents up is, is our number one goal.

And it's, it's the market survey page for permanent Q, I swear, I'm, I'm interested to see how many logins I have because I'm using it daily um multiple times a day because it's just such a great way when you go to the metrics and kind of see where annually all it, it used to take me hours to ana analyze, um,
like where the comps are with the concession and all that and I can do it in seconds now.
So I'm, I'm just very happy with the whole, the whole program.
I'm glad to hear that.
So it sounds like we're definitely saving some time.
How are we feeling about the accuracy of the data?
That's always something I like to check in with.
Uh especially since for a lot of folks coming on to apartment IQ perhaps from other products.
The difference is not having that shared uh information directly from another competitor.
It's coming from a website.
Have you had any friction or heard anything about that change?
At least in the data source?
No, honestly, no, there was one time I was like, oh, it's not showing this low floor plan for us on there.
But um I, I might have been a user error.
Um It could have been just rented or it was rent, it came live.
You know, it could have been like the timing on when the unit actually hit the website.
But yeah, no, I haven't had any, any issues.
Ok.
Glad to hear it.
If there's ever anything.
Of course, I, you know where to reach our support team, you know how to escalate anything to me if there's anything that's more time pressing.
Uh And of course we have that So just an update feature which goes right to our data integrity team.
So it kind of skips a level of filtering and goes right to the folks who can affect change.
So, um as far as having occupancy up rents up, what are one of the easiest ways to measure that or are you literally just making sure to see like, hey, advertised occupancy, is that going up or down?
Are you using exposure percentages to factor into your occupancies overall?
Are you literally just saying, what are we looking at this month for occupancy?
And how does that compare to last month or this month?
Last year?
Yeah, I mean, I look at it weekly, the percent change in current futures and lease every week.
Um So when I see, you know, a big delta in the wrong direction or even in the right direction, I know that there's maybe something to do with price.
So as as soon as I see a big red or a big green, I immediately will start, you know, digging in a little bit, but I do that weekly.
OK.
Got it.
And our concessions being uh playing a major role or your markets being less driven by constructions and more.
Is it pure pricing?
Um No, so we don't offer upfront concessions.
We will do loss leaders where we'll kind of annualize it.
And just because we're not selling any of our buildings anytime soon, so we don't really need to have our rent rule be,
you know, the top dollar.
Um which is why people put upfront concessions.
We've seen issues with people giving upfront concessions, they get the concession and stop paying rent.
So that's why we kind of have done away with it.
Um All together, we'd rather, you know, just have a little bit lower of a number on a rent roll instead of um, risking that.
Um, but I, I feel like, you know, I would say 20% of the market has calmed down on concessions.
You know, I would say probably 40% is still the same as it was a couple months ago.
And then, you know, the last chunk is, it's gotten more.
So it's, it's different from what we're kind of, yeah, we, we're spread out all over the southeast, so it's, it's really different.
So that's why again, it is a permanent IQ helps.
And I also don't have to, you know, rely on the weekly market survey that um the team puts together um based on accuracy and I usually don't get to rent until later in the week.
Right?
